raw_consumer_statement:
Paid my XXXX credit card bill, check was received and damaged when opened by Citi bank. They returned the check with the original submitted payment statement, undamaged with a letter dated XX/XX/XXXX. I received on XX/XX/XXXX and reissued a check in the amount due {$2500.00} sent by mail that day. Additionally I called the customer service number and spoke with a representative who assured me I would not be charged a late fee or interest on this amount. I received my XXXX statement on XX/XX/XXXX which included {$30.00} late fee and {$97.00} interest charge along with the {$2500.00} still owed and the new charges. Today my credit card was suspended and non usable. I have called and spoken with a supervisor who guarantees that we waived by late fee of {$30.00} and the interest charge of {$97.00} but required that I make a payment over the phone using my bank account information ( which I am NOT comfortable doing ) for the minimum required {$41.00} in order to activate my card. 

How is Citi bank allowed to hold me responsible financially for their mistake!!!
